title: Smoky Roasted French Cheddar Gratin
description: A rich, gooey French gratin that marries smoky cheddar, tandoor heat, and a hint of Indian spice-perfect for a highprotein date night.
instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C (400°F). If you have a tandoor or grill pan, preheat it over high heat.
Butter a 2x2" cast‑iron or oven‑safe skillet.
In a bowl, whisk together heavy cream, milk, minced garlic, smoked paprika, tandoori masala, chopped thyme, salt, and pepper until smooth.
Arrange the sliced French cheddar in a single layer in the skillet.
Pour the cream mixture over the cheese, making sure it pools around the edges.
Sprinkle breadcrumbs evenly over the top (optional for crunch).
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and bake for 15 minutes, until edges bubble and the cream thickens.
Remove the skillet and immediately place it on the preheated tandoor or grill pan; cook for 3–4 minutes, or use the oven broiler for 1–2 minutes, until the top develops a smoky, charred crust.
Let the gratin rest for 5 minutes, then serve hot.
